Skip navigation Shop Tickets News Matches Palace TV Crystal palace Crystal palace News Matches Palace TV Teams Shop Tickets Crystal palace Login / Sign up Login / Sign up Glasner reflects on FA Cup anniversary & facing Brentford First-team JUST NOW Tomorrow is 17th May – a date which no Crystal Palace fan, or their FA Cup-winning Manager Oliver Glasner, will surely ever forget. Whilst the Eagles will face Brentford in the afternoon in their final away Premier League game of 2025/26, the day will also one mark one to the year since Palace’s players wrote themselves into club immortality with a 1-0 win over Manchester City at Wembley. Skip navigation Shop Tickets News Matches Palace TV Crystal palace Crystal palace News Matches Palace TV Teams Shop Tickets Crystal palace Login / Sign up Login / Sign up Glasner reflects on FA Cup anniversary & facing Brentford First-team JUST NOW Tomorrow is 17th May – a date which no Crystal Palace fan, or their FA Cup-winning Manager Oliver Glasner, will surely ever forget. Whilst the Eagles will face Brentford in the afternoon in their final away Premier League game of 2025/26, the day will also one mark one to the year since Palace’s players wrote themselves into club immortality with a 1-0 win over Manchester City at Wembley. Share facebook x email Leeds United will face Brighton & Hove Albion in their final Premier League fixture on home soil this season at the weekend, knowing their top-flight status is already secure for next campaign. That confirmation came after West Ham United were beaten by Arsenal last time out but, despite safety being achieved, Daniel Farke’s Whites still showed great character to earn a point against Tottenham Hotspur on Monday evening. In our discussion with the boss, we looked back at a terrific first season back in the top-flight, highlighting key moments and the unwavering support of the Elland Road faithful. registryArticleSamples.title: Preview: Leeds United vs Brighton & Hove Albion
registryArticleSamples.textPreview: Preview: Leeds United vs Brighton & Hove Albion - Leeds United leedsunited.com leedsunited.com Tickets LUTV News Tickets & Hospitality Men Women Academy Shop Events More... search trigger burger menu Previous Next Mens Preview: Leeds United vs Brighton & Hove Albion 16 May 26 Whites take on Seagulls at Elland Road. Share facebook x email Leeds United will face Brighton & Hove Albion in their final Premier League fixture on home soil this season at the weekend, knowing their top-flight status is already secure for next campaign. That confirmation came after West Ham United were beaten by Arsenal last time out but, despite safety being achieved, Daniel Farke’s Whites still showed great character to earn a point against Tottenham Hotspur on Monday evening. Behind in the capital, England international Dominic Calvert-Lewin made it 14 goals in all competitions as he despatched a second-half p

registryArticleSamples.textPreview: Buy your matchday programme against Brighton & Hove Albion - Leeds United leedsunited.com leedsunited.com Tickets LUTV News Tickets & Hospitality Men Women Academy Shop Events More... search trigger burger menu Previous Next Retail Buy your matchday programme against Brighton & Hove Albion 15 May 26 Available around the ground or online. Share facebook x email Having guaranteed their Premier League status with games to spare, who better to be the exclusive matchday programme interview than the man who masterminded it, Daniel Farke? In our discussion with the boss, we looked back at a terrific first season back in the top-flight, highlighting key moments and the unwavering support of the Elland Road faithful. registryArticleSamples.textPreview: Leeds United reaffirm zero-tolerance policy on discrimination ahead of Brighton fixture - Leeds United leedsunited.com leedsunited.com Tickets LUTV News Tickets & Hospitality Men Women Academy Shop Events More... search trigger burger menu Previous Next Club Leeds United reaffirm zero-tolerance policy on discrimination ahead of Brighton fixture 15 May 26 Don’t discriminate. Share facebook x email Prior to Sunday’s Premier League meeting with Brighton & Hove Albion, Leeds United would like to remind supporters we have a zero-tolerance policy towards the use of any homophobic or discriminatory language, chanting, abuse or gestures and condemn this behaviour. Elland Road is once again sold out and as always we appreciate your amazing support.
